name: Kids' noise-reducing ear defenders
blurb: Slip these on before a movie, a sporting event, or a loud attraction. They take the edge off the volume without cutting sound out completely, so your child can still hear you.
useCase: Loud movies, sporting events, fireworks, and busy attractions

name: Noise-cancelling headphones for kids
blurb: For older kids who'd rather have quiet or their own music on a hard day out — active noise cancelling muffles more of the background hum of a mall or arena.
useCase: Older kids on long, loud days out — malls, arenas, travel

name: Low-profile reusable ear plugs
blurb: Discreet, reusable ear plugs that lower the volume of the world a notch without blocking it out — handy for tweens and teens who don't want bulky earmuffs.
useCase: Older kids and parents who want subtle, packable sound relief

name: Foldable noise-reduction earmuffs (teen & adult sizes)
blurb: A larger, higher-reduction earmuff for bigger kids, teens, and parents — folds flat to keep in a bag for unexpectedly loud places.
useCase: Teens and grown-ups at concerts, fireworks, and loud venues
search: foldable noise reduction ear muffs adult

name: Kids' sunglasses
blurb: Bright, fluorescent-lit stores and arenas are a common trigger. A simple pair of shades can make an indoor space far easier to tolerate.
useCase: Bright, fluorescent-lit stores and arenas

name: Wide-brim or bucket hat
blurb: Cuts overhead glare and harsh lighting — useful indoors under bright lights as well as outside in the sun.
useCase: Cutting overhead glare indoors and sun outdoors

name: Galaxy star projector night light
blurb: Projects slow-moving stars and color across the ceiling — a calming visual focus for winding down a bedroom or a quiet corner.
useCase: Calming a bedroom or quiet space at the end of the day

name: Fiber-optic color-changing lamp
blurb: Soft, shifting strands of color give eyes something gentle to follow. A low-key visual that many kids find soothing to hold and watch.
useCase: A gentle visual focus during calm-down time
search: fiber optic color changing sensory lamp

name: Weighted lap pad
blurb: A few pounds across the lap can be grounding during a movie, a haircut, or a long wait. Small enough to keep in your bag for outings.
useCase: Haircuts, movies, restaurants, and long waits

name: Kids' weighted blanket
blurb: Gentle, even pressure that can help with winding down at home or resetting after an overwhelming day. Choose a weight around 10% of your child's body weight.
useCase: Winding down at home or resetting after a big day

name: Weighted stuffed animal
blurb: A huggable comfort object with a calming bit of weight — easy to bring along for car rides, waiting rooms, and brand-new places.
useCase: Car rides, waiting rooms, and new places

name: Chewable sensory necklace
blurb: A safe, discreet outlet for kids who chew to self-regulate, so sleeves and collars get a break. Handy in the car and during outings.
useCase: Kids who chew to self-regulate; car rides and outings

name: Weighted compression vest
blurb: An adjustable vest that gives a steady, hug-like pressure some kids find grounding while sitting, working, or transitioning between activities.
useCase: Steady deep-pressure input during seated or focus time
search: weighted compression vest for kids sensory

name: Weighted shoulder wrap
blurb: Rests gently across the shoulders for a calming bit of pressure during homework, car rides, or a tough moment — easy to drape on and take off.
useCase: Quick, grounding pressure during homework or travel

name: Sensory body sock
blurb: A stretchy full-body sack kids climb inside to push, stretch, and feel resistance — a fun way to get calming deep-pressure and body-awareness input at home.
useCase: Active deep-pressure play and body awareness at home

name: Doorway sensory swing
blurb: A cozy cocoon swing that hangs in a doorway or frame, giving gentle motion and a wrapped, snug feeling many kids seek out to settle.
useCase: Calming motion and a snug retreat indoors

name: Wiggle (wobble) seat cushion
blurb: An inflatable cushion that lets a child wiggle and shift while staying seated — helpful for kids who focus better with a little movement.
useCase: Staying seated at meals, homework, or circle time

name: Mini toddler trampoline with handle
blurb: A sturdy little trampoline with a support handle for safe bouncing indoors — a good outlet for kids who need to move to feel regulated.
useCase: Burning energy and getting movement input indoors
search: mini trampoline for kids indoor handle

name: Wooden balance / wobble board
blurb: A simple curved board for rocking, balancing, and open-ended play that builds coordination and gives the body the movement it craves.
useCase: Balance, coordination, and active play

name: Sensory crash pad
blurb: A soft, padded landing zone for kids who love to jump and crash — a safer way to get big deep-pressure input and active play at home.
useCase: Safe jumping, crashing, and deep-pressure play

name: Scooter board with handles
blurb: A classic gym scooter with safe-grip handles for floor play that builds core strength and coordination — a favorite for movement breaks.
useCase: Indoor movement breaks and core-strengthening play

name: Chewable pencil toppers
blurb: Designed by a speech-language pathologist, these slip onto a pencil to give a safe outlet for kids who chew on pens and sleeves during schoolwork.
useCase: Kids who chew on pencils and clothing at school

name: Textured handheld chew
blurb: A medical-grade, textured chew tool that gives oral sensory input for kids who chew to stay calm and focused — easy to keep in a pocket or bag.
useCase: On-the-go oral input for kids who chew to self-regulate

name: Silicone chew bracelet
blurb: A discreet, wearable chew that looks like a regular bracelet — a low-profile option for older kids who'd rather not wear a necklace.
useCase: Discreet chewing support for older kids

name: Quiet fidget toy set
blurb: Pocket-sized, low-noise fidgets give busy hands something to do during a show, a meal out, or a wait — without distracting everyone nearby.
useCase: Shows, meals out, and waits — without distracting others

name: Fidget rings & bracelets
blurb: Discreet self-regulation tools that don't look out of place at school or on a day out, so an older child can fidget without feeling singled out.
useCase: Discreet self-regulation at school or out

name: Jumbo pop-it fidget
blurb: A big, satisfying bubble-popping mat that resets endlessly — simple, repetitive input that lots of kids find calming and absorbing.
useCase: Repetitive, satisfying fidgeting for calm and focus

name: Infinity cube
blurb: A pocket cube that folds over itself endlessly — a quiet, one-handed fidget that's easy to use under a desk or in a waiting room.
useCase: Quiet one-handed fidgeting at a desk or out

name: Tangle fidget (3-pack)
blurb: Twistable, connected segments to bend and turn — a near-silent fidget that's great for kids who like to keep their hands busy while listening.
useCase: Silent hand-busy fidgeting while listening or learning

name: Monkey noodles (stretchy strings)
blurb: Brightly colored strings that stretch several feet and snap back — a stretchy, tactile fidget for pulling, wrapping, and squeezing.
useCase: Stretchy, tactile fidgeting for busy hands

name: Mesh & marble fidgets
blurb: A marble slides up and down inside a mesh tube — a quiet, repetitive motion that's easy to do one-handed and hard to put down.
useCase: Quiet, repetitive fidgeting in class or on the go

name: Pop tubes
blurb: Bendy tubes that stretch, click, and connect — part fidget, part building toy, with a satisfying sound and feel kids return to again and again.
useCase: Tactile, hands-on fidget play and quiet building

name: Fidget pen
blurb: A real pen with a built-in fidget, so an older kid can keep hands busy in class or at the table without an obvious toy.
useCase: Discreet fidgeting for tweens, teens, and homework

name: Liquid motion bubbler
blurb: Colored droplets drift slowly down through the chambers — a mesmerizing visual that gives kids something calming to watch and reset with.
useCase: A calming visual focus during a reset

name: Therapy putty set
blurb: Stretch, squeeze, and pull through a set of resistances — hands-on input that's both calming and good for building hand strength.
useCase: Hands-on calming input and hand strengthening

name: Expandable breathing ball
blurb: Opens wide and shrinks back down, giving kids a visual to breathe along with — a simple, screen-free way to practice slow breaths together.
useCase: Practicing slow breathing with a visual guide

name: Breathing exercise cards
blurb: Illustrated cards walk kids through a handful of simple breathing exercises — easy prompts for a calm-down corner or a tough moment.

name: Squishy stress balls (set)
blurb: A set of squeezable balls in different textures for hands that need to squeeze and release — pocketable for school, the car, or out and about.
useCase: Squeeze-and-release calming for busy or anxious hands

name: Light-up glitter sensory tubes
blurb: Glitter swirls slowly through a glowing tube — a soothing visual that doubles as a gentle 'watch it settle' timer for transitions.
useCase: A calming visual and gentle transition timer

name: Pop-up blackout sensory tent
blurb: A small, dim retreat a child can crawl into when the world is too much — a portable 'quiet corner' for home, complete with soft lighting.
useCase: A cozy quiet retreat for decompressing at home
search: pop up blackout sensory tent for kids

name: Kinetic sand
blurb: Moldable sand that holds together and never dries out — a satisfying tactile activity that keeps hands happily busy at the table.
useCase: Calming, hands-on tabletop sensory play

name: Water beads (non-toxic)
blurb: Squishy, slippery beads for scooping and squeezing in a sensory bin — a favorite tactile activity. Best for older kids with supervision (small parts).
useCase: Supervised tactile sensory-bin play for older kids

name: Spiky tactile / massage balls
blurb: Bumpy balls to roll, squeeze, and press — gentle tactile and deep-pressure input that's easy to keep in a calm-down kit or backpack.
useCase: Tactile input and gentle deep-pressure rolling

name: Montessori busy board
blurb: Latches, zippers, and switches all on one board — hands-on practice for everyday skills that keeps little fingers busy and engaged.
useCase: Hands-on fine-motor and life-skills play

name: Magnetic visual schedule
blurb: Picture magnets lay out the day step by step so kids know what's next — a predictable routine that takes the surprise out of transitions.
useCase: Making daily routines and transitions predictable

name: Feelings & emotions poster
blurb: A clear visual of different feelings so kids can point to and name how they feel — a gentle way to build emotional vocabulary together.
useCase: Helping kids identify and share how they feel

name: First-Then visual board
blurb: A simple 'first this, then that' board with picture cards that makes expectations clear and eases the move from a task to a reward.
useCase: Easing transitions with clear first-then expectations

name: Emotion flash cards
blurb: A deck of clear feeling faces for talking through emotions and social situations — handy for quiet practice at home or in the car.
useCase: Building emotional and social vocabulary

name: Portable white noise machine
blurb: A pocket-sized machine that masks unpredictable background noise with steady sound — useful for sleep, hotel rooms, and noisy travel.
useCase: Masking unpredictable noise for sleep and travel
search: portable white noise machine for kids

name: Blackout bed canopy / tent
blurb: A breathable canopy that darkens and encloses the bed into a calm, den-like space — comforting for kids who feel safer in a smaller, dimmer spot.
useCase: A darker, enclosed, calming sleep space

name: Sensory compression bed sheet
blurb: A stretchy sheet that hugs the mattress and the child inside for light, even pressure — a cooler, lighter alternative to a weighted blanket.
useCase: Light, breathable deep-pressure for settling at night

name: Seamless sensory socks
blurb: Truly seamless socks with no toe ridge to rub or annoy — a small change that heads off a lot of morning meltdowns for sock-sensitive kids.
useCase: Kids bothered by sock seams and toe ridges

name: Tagless soft tee
blurb: A super-soft, tagless shirt with flat, covered seams — designed to remove the scratchy spots that make ordinary clothes unbearable for some kids.
useCase: Kids sensitive to tags, seams, and stiff fabric

name: Divided suction plates
blurb: Soft silicone plates with separated sections that suction to the table — keeping foods from touching (and from sliding away) at mealtimes.
useCase: Mealtimes where foods can't touch and plates slide

name: Visual countdown timer
blurb: Seeing time pass makes a transition feel predictable — 'when the red is gone, we leave.' Great for easing the end of a fun activity.
useCase: Making transitions predictable; ending fun activities

name: Sensory kit bag
blurb: A small dedicated bag keeps headphones, fidgets, sunglasses, and a lap pad packed and ready, so a tough moment doesn't turn into a scramble.
useCase: Keeping the whole sensory kit packed and ready

name: Communication picture cards
blurb: Picture cards give a non-speaking or overwhelmed child a fast way to ask for a break, the bathroom, or home when words are hard.
useCase: Fast requests when words are hard, on the go

name: Sand timer set
blurb: A set of colorful sand timers for a few common lengths — a quiet, screen-free way to show how long is left for a turn, a task, or a break.
useCase: Showing 'how much longer' without a screen
search: sand timer set for kids classroom

name: Car-seat travel tray
blurb: A sturdy lap tray that turns a car seat into a steady surface for snacks, drawing, and fidgets — a lifesaver on longer drives and waits.
useCase: Long car rides and keeping activities within reach
search: car seat travel tray for kids

name: Toddler harness backpack
blurb: A cute backpack with a detachable tether so a child can explore in crowded places while staying close — reassuring for kids who may bolt.
useCase: Crowded outings — zoos, airports, festivals
search: toddler backpack with safety harness leash

name: Door & window safety alarms
blurb: Simple stick-on alarms that chime when a door or window opens — peace of mind at home for families of kids who wander or elope.
useCase: Home peace of mind for kids who wander
search: door window safety alarm for kids

name: Kids GPS smartwatch
blurb: A kid-friendly watch with location and two-way calling — a way to stay connected and locate your child on busy outings, no phone required.
useCase: Staying connected and locating kids on outings

name: Autism medical ID bracelet
blurb: A comfortable, waterproof ID band noting your child is autistic and may not respond — helpful information for first responders or a helpful stranger.
useCase: Outings where a child may get separated or not respond
search: autism medical ID bracelet for kids

name: FL-41 tinted light-sensitivity glasses
blurb: Rose-tinted FL-41 lenses are made for the kind of light that triggers sensory overload — the flicker and glare of fluorescent and LED lighting. Gentler than sunglasses indoors, they take the harsh edge off bright stores, classrooms, and screens.
useCase: Fluorescent and LED glare indoors, classrooms, and screens

name: Fluorescent light filter covers
blurb: Magnetic fabric panels that fit over standard ceiling light fixtures and soften the harsh glare and flicker that many kids find overwhelming. A calmer-feeling classroom, bedroom, or therapy room without changing the bulbs.
useCase: Softening overhead fluorescent or LED glare in a room

name: Weighted blackout sleep mask
blurb: A gently weighted, fully blackout eye mask that adds a little calming pressure while blocking out every bit of light. Packs flat for the car, the plane, or an unfamiliar hotel room where the light is never quite right.
useCase: Calming, total-darkness rest at home or while traveling

name: Sunrise wake-up light alarm clock
blurb: Instead of a jarring buzz, the light brightens slowly like a sunrise so waking up is gradual rather than a shock to the system. Doubles as a dimmable night light and sound machine for a predictable bedtime routine.
useCase: Gentle, gradual waking and a calmer bedtime routine
search: sunrise wake up light alarm clock kids

name: Sensory peanut therapy ball
blurb: The peanut shape rolls forward and back but not side to side, so it's far more stable than a round ball — kids can bounce, rock, and roll for the deep-pressure and movement input that helps them regulate and refocus.
useCase: Active movement and proprioceptive input at home or therapy

name: Vibrating oral sensory chew kit
blurb: A handheld vibrating tool with soft, textured swap-on tips for kids who seek strong oral input. The gentle vibration and chewable textures give a safe outlet for mouthing and can support oral-motor and feeding work.
useCase: Kids who crave intense oral input or oral-motor support

name: Portable travel blackout blind
blurb: Suction-cup blackout panel that sticks to any window and turns a bright hotel room or grandparent's spare room into a properly dark sleeping space. Velcro edges adjust to the window and the whole thing folds into its own travel bag.
useCase: Blacking out unfamiliar rooms on trips and overnights
search: portable blackout blind suction cups travel

name: Bluetooth sleep headphones headband
blurb: A soft fabric headband with flat speakers tucked inside, so a child can fall asleep to white noise, a favorite story, or calm music without hard earbuds pressing in — comfortable even for side sleepers and easy to wash.
useCase: Winding down to calming sound at bedtime or on the go

name: Calm strips (textured sensory stickers)
blurb: Reusable, gritty-textured strips that stick to a phone, desk, or water bottle for a discreet bit of tactile input. A quiet way to rub or scratch out nervous energy that doesn't disturb anyone nearby.
useCase: Discreet tactile regulation at school, desks, and outings
